title: Linux Essentials
description: Linux Essentials practice deck with concise foundational Linux questions for study and exam sessions.
domains: The Linux Community and a Career in Open Source
Finding Your Way on a Linux System
The Power of the Command Line

questions.domain: The Linux Community and a Career in Open Source
questions.question: What best describes open-source software?
answers: Software whose source code is available to inspect and improve
Software sold only by hardware vendors
Software that runs only on Linux
questions.explanation: Open-source software makes its source code available so users and developers can study, modify, and share it.

questions.domain: The Linux Community and a Career in Open Source
questions.question: What is a Linux distribution?
answers: A package of the Linux kernel with tools and software
A command used to install programs
questions.explanation: A distribution combines the Linux kernel with utilities, package management, and applications.

questions.domain: The Linux Community and a Career in Open Source
questions.question: Which organization is known for stewarding the Linux kernel project?
questions.explanation: The Linux Foundation supports the Linux ecosystem and hosts many important open-source projects.

questions.domain: The Linux Community and a Career in Open Source
questions.question: Why are open standards important in the Linux ecosystem?
answers: They help different systems and tools work together
They require all software to be free of charge
They eliminate the need for documentation
questions.explanation: Open standards improve compatibility and interoperability across vendors, systems, and applications.

questions.domain: The Linux Community and a Career in Open Source
questions.question: Which task is commonly associated with an entry-level Linux administrator?
answers: Managing users, packages, and basic system services
Replacing the Linux kernel source code daily
questions.explanation: Entry-level Linux administration often includes user management, software updates, and basic service monitoring.

questions.domain: Finding Your Way on a Linux System
questions.question: Which directory usually contains a user's personal files?
questions.explanation: User home directories are typically stored under /home.

questions.domain: Finding Your Way on a Linux System
questions.question: Which command prints the current working directory?
questions.explanation: The pwd command displays the full path of the current directory.

questions.domain: Finding Your Way on a Linux System
questions.question: Which command shows hidden files in the current directory?
questions.explanation: The -a option makes ls display hidden files, including names beginning with a dot.

questions.domain: Finding Your Way on a Linux System
questions.question: Which directory commonly stores system-wide configuration files?
questions.explanation: The /etc directory contains many system configuration files and service settings.

questions.domain: Finding Your Way on a Linux System
questions.question: Which command is commonly used to read built-in documentation for a Linux command?
questions.explanation: The man command opens manual pages that describe command usage, options, and behavior.

questions.domain: The Power of the Command Line
questions.question: Which command copies a file from one location to another?
questions.explanation: The cp command copies files or directories to a new location.

questions.domain: The Power of the Command Line
questions.question: Which command is used to rename or move a file?
questions.explanation: The mv command can both move a file and rename it.

questions.domain: The Power of the Command Line
questions.question: Which command searches for matching text inside files?
questions.explanation: grep searches input and files for lines that match a pattern.

questions.domain: The Power of the Command Line
questions.question: What does the pipe symbol `|` do in a shell command?
answers: Deletes the output of the first command
Sends the output of one command to another command
questions.explanation: A pipe connects the standard output of one command to the standard input of another.

questions.domain: The Power of the Command Line
questions.question: Which command is commonly used to create or extract `.tar` archives?
questions.explanation: The tar command packages files into archives and can also extract them.

questions.domain: The Linux Operating System
questions.question: Which command commonly shows running processes?
questions.explanation: The ps command displays information about running processes.

questions.domain: The Linux Operating System
questions.question: Which command displays basic system information such as the kernel name?
questions.explanation: uname reports system information, including the kernel name.

questions.domain: The Linux Operating System
questions.question: What is the main purpose of a package manager on Linux?
answers: To install, update, and remove software
questions.explanation: Package managers handle software installation, updates, dependencies, and removal.

questions.domain: The Linux Operating System
questions.question: What is the role of a Linux init system such as systemd?
answers: To manage system startup and services
To compile the kernel from source
questions.explanation: An init system starts the system and manages services and background processes.

questions.domain: The Linux Operating System
questions.question: Which command sends a signal to a process, often to stop it?
questions.explanation: The kill command sends a signal to a process, such as SIGTERM or SIGKILL.

questions.domain: Security and File Permissions
questions.question: Which command changes file permissions?
questions.explanation: chmod modifies file and directory permission bits.

questions.domain: Security and File Permissions
questions.question: Which command changes the owner of a file?
questions.explanation: chown changes file ownership and can also update the group owner.

questions.domain: Security and File Permissions
questions.question: Which command is used to change a user's password?
questions.explanation: The passwd command updates a user's password.

questions.domain: Security and File Permissions
questions.question: What is the main purpose of the `sudo` command?
answers: To run a command with elevated privileges
questions.explanation: sudo allows authorized users to run specific commands with elevated privileges.

questions.domain: Security and File Permissions
questions.question: What permission set does the numeric mode `755` represent for a file?
answers: Owner read/write/execute, group and others read/execute
Owner read only, group write only, others execute only
Owner read/write, group read/write, others no access
questions.explanation: 755 means the owner has rwx and group and others have r-x permissions.
